raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to formally dispute a charge of {$640.00} dated XX/XX/year>, which I have consistently reported as unauthorized and fraudulent. 

In XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX XXXX contacted me regarding a suspicious XXXX transaction. At that time, I clearly stated that I did not make this purchase, and it was identified as potential fraud. As a result, my card was closed and reissued, and I was informed that an investigation would be conducted. 

Since then, I have made multiple follow-up calls regarding this charge. I am now being told that Gap Card Services has proof of the transaction, including an email address and phone number allegedly linked to the purchase of XXXX XXXX tickets. However, I have not been provided with this documentation ( it is in the mail ), and regardless of what was used, I state unequivocally : I did not authorize this transaction I did not purchase any tickets through XXXX  I did not receive any tickets or goods I did not use any tickets or services related to this charge Any email address, phone number, or account associated with this transaction was used without my authorization. 

I am extremely frustrated that this matter continues to be mischaracterized despite my repeated reports. This is a clear case of fraud, and I will not accept responsibility for a charge I neither authorized nor benefited from.
